title: cuscuta abyssinica f. breviloba
intro: Cuscuta abyssinica f. breviloba is a subspecies of the Dodder plant (Cuscuta spp.), a parasitic flowering plant that grows by attaching itself to the stems of other plants to obtain nutrients. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Cuscuta abyssinica f. breviloba include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and alleviating symptoms of fever and rheumatism. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules, and are typically consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
